# Adamant Protocol

> Adamant is a Layer 1 blockchain protocol designed to combine privacy by default, sub-second finality, post-quantum cryptographic security, phone-verifiable correctness, and credibly neutral governance — with no foundation, no premine, no admin keys, and no upgrade authority after genesis. The protocol uses standard, peer-reviewed cryptographic primitives integrated into a single coherent architecture, with credible neutrality enforced at genesis.

Adamant is currently in specification phase. The whitepaper draft is v0.1, published for public review. No chain is running yet.

## Key concepts and terminology

- **Node Runners** (validators in the whitepaper): operators of the active set, 200 at a time. They run Mysticeti-based DAG consensus, publishing one vertex per 250ms round, each referencing 2N/3+1 of the prior round. Order emerges from the structure; commit waves finalise blocks every 4 rounds. Anyone with stake can become a Node Runner without permission.
- **Node Watchers** (light verifiers in the whitepaper): anyone with a smartphone or consumer device. They download a 5–10 KB recursive zero-knowledge proof and verify the entire chain history in 50–200 ms — no full node, no RPC trust, no light-client trust assumption. Verification is cryptographic, not statistical.
- **Service Nodes** (whitepaper §9.10): permissionless, standardised infrastructure providers. Wallets and applications query them for state via a standard query format. Anyone can run one.
- **ADM**: native token. 100,000,000 ADM genesis pool drained over a five-year launch phase via two acquisition paths. Post-launch issuance is 4% → 3% → 2% → 1% per year, in perpetuity, paid only to Node Runners and their delegators. Base fees burned via EIP-1559-style mechanism.
- **Genesis pool**: a fixed 100M ADM supply distributed via burn-to-mint (70M, anyone burning BTC or ETH at a verifiable null address) and Node Runner block rewards (30M). Unclaimed tokens at year 5 are destroyed.
- **Credible neutrality** (Principle I, whitepaper §2.1): the protocol admits no party with the unilateral capability to alter its operation. No on-chain governance, no foundation, no admin keys, no upgrade authority, no premine, no founder allocation. Change requires socially-coordinated forks where Node Runners individually opt in to new client software.

## Design principles

The protocol commits to seven principles in priority order: (1) credible neutrality, (2) privacy by default, (3) verifiability without trust, (4) performance sufficient for use, (5) mutability as a property of objects (not the chain), (6) standard primitives and novel synthesis (no new cryptography), (7) permissionless participation.

## The seven properties

The protocol's contribution is the systems-level synthesis of seven properties no other chain combines: 200,000+ TPS with sub-second finality (Mysticeti, 2024), programmable privacy (Aztec, 2025), encrypted mempool in production (Shutter, 2024), phone-verifiable correctness (Mina, 2021), native account abstraction (zkSync/StarkNet/Aptos, 2022–24), post-quantum signatures (NIST FIPS 204, 2024), and credibly neutral governance (Bitcoin/Monero, 2009/2014). No single chain in the present landscape combines more than three.

## Pages

- [Home](https://adamantprotocol.com/): Overview of the protocol, the seven properties no chain combines, and the five mechanisms Adamant uses to deliver them.
- [Introduction](https://adamantprotocol.com/about): Plain-English introduction. Four core properties, framings against familiar systems, intended users, what the protocol is not.
- [Genesis](https://adamantprotocol.com/genesis): Launch mechanism, the fixed 100M ADM genesis pool, two acquisition paths, post-launch issuance schedule, what is not happening at launch.
- [Specification](https://adamantprotocol.com/spec): Whitepaper draft v0.1 in summary form. Reading-friendly index of the 13 sections, with deep links to the canonical source.

## Whitepaper (canonical source)

The reference whitepaper lives in the protocol repository:

- [Abstract](https://github.com/adamant-protocol/adamant/blob/main/whitepaper/00-abstract.md): One-page summary of the protocol.
- [Introduction & Motivation](https://github.com/adamant-protocol/adamant/blob/main/whitepaper/01-introduction.md): The state of the art in 2026, the gap that remains, and Adamant's thesis.
- [Design Principles](https://github.com/adamant-protocol/adamant/blob/main/whitepaper/02-design-principles.md): The seven principles in priority order.
- [Cryptographic Foundation](https://github.com/adamant-protocol/adamant/blob/main/whitepaper/03-cryptographic-foundation.md): Ed25519, ML-DSA, BLS12-381, SHA-3, Halo 2, threshold encryption.
- [Identity & Accounts](https://github.com/adamant-protocol/adamant/blob/main/whitepaper/04-identity-accounts.md): Native account abstraction, view keys, selective disclosure.
- [Object Model & State](https://github.com/adamant-protocol/adamant/blob/main/whitepaper/05-object-model-state.md): Object-based state, mutability variants, ownership modes.
- [Execution & VM](https://github.com/adamant-protocol/adamant/blob/main/whitepaper/06-execution-vm.md): Adamant Move language, multi-dimensional gas, mutability annotations.
- [Privacy Layer](https://github.com/adamant-protocol/adamant/blob/main/whitepaper/07-privacy-layer.md): Halo 2 circuits, shielded transactions, view keys.
- [Consensus](https://github.com/adamant-protocol/adamant/blob/main/whitepaper/08-consensus.md): Mysticeti-based DAG, threshold-encrypted mempool, recursive proofs.
- [Networking & Mempool](https://github.com/adamant-protocol/adamant/blob/main/whitepaper/09-networking-mempool.md): libp2p networking, encrypted mempool, service-node infrastructure market.
- [Economics & Incentives](https://github.com/adamant-protocol/adamant/blob/main/whitepaper/10-economics-incentives.md): Genesis pool, post-launch issuance, fee burn, validator-funded infrastructure.
- [Genesis & Constitution](https://github.com/adamant-protocol/adamant/blob/main/whitepaper/11-genesis-constitution.md): What is fixed at genesis. The structural absence of any mechanism to modify the protocol.
- [Conclusion & Open Problems](https://github.com/adamant-protocol/adamant/blob/main/whitepaper/12-conclusion.md): What remains to be done, acknowledged constraints.

## Repository

- [GitHub: adamant-protocol/adamant](https://github.com/adamant-protocol/adamant): Reference implementation. Apache 2.0. The implementation is forthcoming; the protocol is in specification phase.
- [GitHub: adamant-protocol/adamant-website](https://github.com/adamant-protocol/adamant-website): Source code for this website.

## Optional

- [Updates](https://adamantprotocol.com/updates): Project updates and design notes.
- [llms-full.txt](https://adamantprotocol.com/llms-full.txt): Extended index with inlined content of the marketing pages and key whitepaper sections.
